---
title: "Assignment 9"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-04"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Import required libraries 
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

## Assignment 9 - Determinants II and Project Proposal

### Preliminary Data Analysis 

#### Ballot Complexity and Voter Turnout

Using data from CEDA (California Elections Data Archive) 2024 Dataset 

##### Import the data 
```{r}
setwd("/Users/mayajohnson/Desktop")
measures_2024 <- read_csv("Measures2024.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
candidates_2024 <- read_csv("Candidates2024.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

head(ca_2024)
```

##### Find the ballot complexity (how many types per county for each election (grouped by date))
 - To find ballot complexity/length, I grouped the ballots by County and Date of election, and counted for each how many distinct ID's there are for each County/Date. 
 
```{r}
ballot_length <- ca_2024 %>%
  group_by(CNTYNAME, DATE) %>%
  summarise(ballot_length = n_distinct(MeasID))
```

##### Find the voter turnout for each election (grouped by date) and county 
- To find voter turnout, I counted the number of votes per County/Date group. 
```{r}
# Extract turnout from the candidates data set 
turnout <- candidates_2024 %>%
  group_by(CNTYNAME, DATE) %>%
  summarise(turnout = max(TOTVOTES, na.rm = TRUE))
```

##### Combine the data and make the scatterplot 
```{r}
# Combine the data sets 
analysis_df <- left_join(ballot_length, turnout, by = c("CNTYNAME","DATE")) %>% filter(turnout < 2000000)   # I trimmed this to remove a very large outlier 

# Make the scatter plot 
ggplot(analysis_df, aes(x = ballot_length, y = turnout)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) + # I made the dots transparent because a lot overlapped 
  geom_smooth(method=lm) +
  scale_y_log10() + # The values were all over the place for voter turnout so make it log values instead helps make the graph trend more readable 
  labs(
    title = "Ballot Complexity and Voter Turnout",
    subtitle = "In Various 2024 California Ballots", 
    x = "Number of Ballot Measures",
    y = "Total Ballots Cast (Log Values)"
  )
```
